items.title: India-Russia pact allows troops and warships on each other's soil
Middle East crisis live: Hegseth says US-Iran ceasefire ‘is not over’ despite growing tensions in strait of Hormuz
items.description: US defence secretary tells press Iran is attempting ‘international extortion’ but the ceasefire remains in placeDonald Trump sends warships to break Iran’s strait of Hormuz blockadeIran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, is travelling to Beijing later today for talks with his Chinese counterpart “on bilateral relation…

items.title: Several killed in Russian strikes on Ukraine ahead of rival ceasefires
items.description: Russian drone and missile strikes targeting Ukraine’s power grid during the night killed at least five people and wounded 39 others, Ukrainian authorities said Tuesday, less than a day before Kyiv said that it would enact a ceasefire and three days before Moscow promised its own pause in hostilities.

items.title: Why Europe’s car industry is at the centre of a new US trade war
items.description: Trump has threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks exported to the US from 15 percent to 25 percent.

items.title: UAE Accuses Iran Of Hitting Oil Sector, World Leaders Condemn Dangerous Escalation
items.description: Several countries have condemned Iran and called an attack on oil facilities in the United Arab Emirates a dangerous escalation. Thick smoke was filmed pouring out from the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone that the UAE claimed was hit by an Iranian drone attack on May 4. This comes as both the US and Iran fired shots in the …

items.title: Ahmed Fouad Alkhatib: Israel has re-embraced Hamas rule in Gaza
items.description: Describing a rat-infested Gaza Strip and further entrenchment of the terror organization, the head of Realign for Palestine weighs in on the lack of momentum with the Board of Peace plan The post Ahmed Fouad Alkhatib: Israel has re-embraced Hamas rule in Gaza appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: How Colombia became a top exporter of mercenaries
items.description: Every year Colombia retires thousands of battle-hardened veterans from its armed forces. Many end up enlisting in foreign conflicts.

items.title: U.K. Targets Russian Army Recruitment Network and Drone Suppliers in Latest Sanctions Package
items.description: The new measures target what British officials condemned as a “barbaric” network that lures foreign nationals to Russia with the promise of work, only to force them into frontline combat in Ukraine or military production.

items.title: Trump Calls Zelensky ‘Tricky Guy’ but Says He ‘Likes’ Him
items.description: US President Donald Trump said he supports a negotiated settlement in the ongoing Russo-Ukrainian War, despite continued battlefield losses for Kyiv.

items.title: Zelensky, 'cinico attaccarci nei giorni che precedono una tregua di propaganda'
Russia declares a truce in Ukraine to mark Victory Day
items.description: Russia declared a unilateral ceasefire in Ukraine for Friday and Saturday to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, but threatened to strike back at Kyiv if it tries to disrupt festivities.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine declare separate truces
items.description: Moscow announced a May 8–9 truce tied to WWII commemorations and warned of 'massive missile strike' if breached, while Kyiv called the proposal unserious and set its own ceasefire beginning May 6.

items.title: Russia offers Ukraine May 8-9 ceasefire amid WWII anniversary, but threatens major reprisals in same breath
items.description: President Putin has unilaterally declared a two-day truce amid commemorations of the end of World War II in Moscow. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy proposed his own truce starting a couple of days earlier.

items.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
items.description: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

items.title: Middle East: Fighting and displacement in Lebanon; call for ‘sustained access’ in Gaza
items.description: Lebanon’s fragile ceasefire is being tested by renewed violence and rising humanitarian needs amid continuing civilians displacement, services under pressure and aid cuts.
